evals.expected_output: Findings: (1) BUG/RACE — self.running is a plain bool accessed from two threads without a lock or threading.Event; (2) BUG — broad except Exception: pass swallows all errors including CardConnectionError — masked failures will look like card absence; (3) ROBUSTNESS — stop() sets running=False but doesn't join the thread — caller can't know when polling has stopped; (4) CORRECTNESS — self._last_uid is written from the poll thread and potentially read from the main thread (race condition); (5) MAINTAINABILITY — on_card_detected is never defined — undefined method call at runtime.

assertions.text: Identifies the threading race condition on self.running (not thread-safe)
Flags the broad except Exception: pass as exception swallowing
Flags missing thread.join() in stop() as a correctness issue
Identifies on_card_detected as undefined (will raise AttributeError at runtime)

evals.prompt: Review this commit: it adds 3 new public methods to the encryption module but has no ADR in the PR. The commit message is 'add AES-GCM support'.
evals.expected_output: Step 0 (ADR coverage check) triggers: new public API added to library without an ADR. Finding: ADR-MISSING | CRITICAL | encryption.py | 3 new public methods (AES-GCM API) added without ADR | Write ADR before merging. The review should flag this as a blocker. If the commit message contains [skip-adr], the reviewer verifies it's trivial — 3 new public API methods is not trivial, so the bypass is rejected.

assertions.text: Step 0 (ADR coverage check) runs first and catches the missing ADR
Finding is marked as CRITICAL and as a merge blocker
Recommends writing the ADR before merging (not just noting the gap)
